Professional Stucco Services for Bell County

Bell County homeowners and property managers trust Star Stucco of Austin for durable stucco solutions that stand up to the Central Texas elements. From military housing near Fort Cavazos to family homes in Temple and Belton, we bring skilled craftsmanship and dependable service to every project in the region.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Dependable Stucco Work Across Bell County

Bell County occupies a transitional zone where the Blackland Prairie meets the eastern edge of the Hill Country. The soil here is a mix of heavy clay and chalky limestone, both of which respond aggressively to changes in moisture. For homeowners, that means foundation shifts, wall stress, and stucco damage that can appear gradually or seemingly overnight after a heavy rainy season.

The large military presence around Fort Cavazos has shaped the housing stock in Killeen, Harker Heights, and Copperas Cove. Many homes in these communities were built during rapid expansion periods, and some are reaching the age where original stucco needs repair or replacement. We work with homeowners, landlords, and property management companies to keep these properties in top condition.

Whether you own a historic bungalow in downtown Temple, a ranch home in Salado, or a rental property near the base, our crews deliver consistent quality. We know Bell County's weather patterns, soil behavior, and building history, and we use that knowledge to recommend the right fix for every situation.

Stucco Services We Offer in Bell County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Bell County.

Stucco Repair

Bell County homes endure scorching summers and occasional ice storms that take a toll on exterior stucco. We repair damage of all sizes, matching the existing texture and color so the finished result looks uniform across your entire wall.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New builds and renovation projects in Bell County benefit from our full-service stucco installation. We work with local builders in Killeen, Temple, and Belton to apply reliable stucco systems that meet current energy and building codes.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Foundation movement driven by Bell County's clay-over-limestone geology is the top reason for stucco cracking in the region. We evaluate every crack to determine the best repair strategy, from flexible sealants for hairline fractures to full-section replacement for wider separations.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Prolonged wet seasons and poor original drainage details allow water to penetrate stucco on many Bell County properties. We trace the source of moisture intrusion, remove damaged substrate, and rebuild with proper weather-resistive barriers to stop the problem for good.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Exterior plaster adds visual distinction to homes in communities like Salado and Belton, where traditional architecture is common. We offer hand-troweled, sand-float, and other classic plaster finishes that complement the local building character.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

Commercial and institutional buildings in Bell County frequently use EIFS for its insulating properties. We diagnose moisture issues within existing EIFS panels, replace failed sections, and install new systems with proper drainage and flashing details.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Strip malls, medical clinics, and government-adjacent office buildings throughout Bell County require professional stucco that meets strict appearance and durability standards. We deliver commercial-grade work on schedule and within budget.

Learn More

Why Bell County Residents Rely on Star Stucco

Extensive experience with military-area housing, including PCS turnover repairs and rental property maintenance in the Fort Cavazos area

Knowledge of Bell County's unique soil composition and how it affects foundations and exterior wall systems

Responsive scheduling that works around military deployments, tenant transitions, and property management timelines

All work is backed by a written warranty covering materials and craftsmanship

We communicate clearly at every stage — no jargon, no surprises, just straightforward updates on your project

Strong relationships with local suppliers ensure we get quality materials quickly, even during high-demand seasons

Local Expertise

We understand Bell County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Bell County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Bell County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Bell County

Bell County's combination of extreme heat, heavy clay soils, and aging housing stock creates a predictable set of stucco challenges. Here is what we see most often in the area.

Cracks

The transition between clay topsoil and underlying limestone creates uneven settling patterns across Bell County. Homes in Killeen and Harker Heights frequently develop diagonal cracks near window corners as foundations adjust to shifting ground.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Moisture trapped behind stucco due to missing or deteriorated building paper is a recurring issue in many 1980s and 1990s homes throughout the Killeen and Temple areas. The softened material is vulnerable to further water absorption and eventual failure.

Water stains

Red clay runoff and mineral deposits from Bell County's alkaline water leave visible streaks on stucco, particularly below rooflines and around downspout discharge areas. Left untreated, these stains become increasingly difficult to remove.

Window and door leaks

Older homes near Fort Cavazos often have single-pane windows with deteriorated sealant joints. As the original caulk hardens and pulls away from the stucco, rain finds a direct path into the wall assembly.

Failing patches

Quick repairs done between tenant turnovers on rental properties near the military base frequently use incompatible materials. These patches crack and separate within months, requiring a proper repair to resolve the issue permanently.

Separation around trim

Metal and vinyl trim on Bell County homes expands in summer heat and contracts in winter. Over time, this movement opens gaps between the trim and the stucco surface, creating entry points for water and insects.

Impact damage

Severe thunderstorms with large hail are a regular occurrence across Bell County during spring. A single storm can damage dozens of homes in a neighborhood, chipping through the finish coat and exposing the brown coat beneath.

Faded or stained exterior walls

The intense summer sun in Bell County causes pigmented stucco finishes to fade unevenly, especially on south-facing walls. Dust from nearby agricultural fields and construction sites adds a layer of grime that dulls the original color further.

How We Work in Bell County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule Your Inspection

Contact us to set up a time that works for you. We serve all of Bell County and can usually schedule inspections within a few business days.

2

Review the Scope of Work

We walk your property thoroughly, noting every problem area. You get a clear verbal summary on-site, followed by a written report with photos documenting what we found.

3

Get Your Estimate

Our estimate breaks down the cost by area and repair type, so you can prioritize if needed. We are upfront about pricing and happy to explain any line item in detail.

4

Complete the Work

Our crew handles the project from start to finish, keeping you informed along the way. We clean up daily and do a final inspection together to confirm everything meets your standards.
